disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The heart races and pounds, then slows right down, and she then goes weak and blacks out
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe what her heart has been doing?
What happens just before she feels faint?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's like a switch — she'll be pounding and racing, then it drops away and she goes limp and dizzy and passes out.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Fainted today while walking to the bathroom and was unconscious for about two minutes
trigger_question_examples: How long was she unconscious for?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — she collapsed on the way to the bathroom this morning and was out cold for a couple of minutes before she came round.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Three months of severe fatigue and getting breathless and exhausted with even light activity
trigger_question_examples: How has her energy been over the last few months?
Can she manage her usual activities?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She's been wiped out for months — can't do the smallest thing without getting puffed and needing to sit down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A new blood pressure tablet (metoprolol) was started recently
trigger_question_examples: Has she started or changed any medications recently?
What is she taking for her blood pressure and when was it started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The doctor put her on a new blood pressure tablet not long ago — metoprolol, a low dose.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm not sure it's anything to do with the tablet, she's just been getting older and frailer.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Feels lightheaded whenever she stands up
trigger_question_examples: Does she feel dizzy when she stands?
Is it worse with position changes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Every time she sits up or stands she goes lightheaded and has to lie straight back down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No crushing chest pain, no sweating, no vomiting with the episodes
trigger_question_examples: Did she have any chest pain or heaviness?
Was she sweaty or clammy or sick with it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, no chest pain or sweating — it's the pounding then the weakness and blacking out, not pain.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She did not hit her head in the fall and knows where she is and what day it is
trigger_question_examples: Did she hit her head when she collapsed?
Is she making sense and oriented now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She didn't bang her head — staff saw her go down onto the fall mat — and she's talking sense, just very slow and weak.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: She is now too weak to get out of bed and is at high risk of falling
trigger_question_examples: How is she managing to move around now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: She can't stand at all now without collapsing — we've got her flat in bed with the rails up and a fall mat down.
